channel.title: NPR Topics: Business
channel.description: Find the latest business news with reports on Wall Street, interest rates, banking, companies, and U.S. and world financial markets. Subscribe to the Business Story of the Day podcast.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: U.S. government to drop tax claims against Trump in broadening of IRS settlement
item.description: As part of the settlement agreement, the U.S. is "forever barred and precluded" from examining or prosecuting President Trump, his sons and the Trump organization's current tax issues, according to a document posted to the DOJ website.
item.content:encoded:
As part of the settlement agreement, the U.S. is "forever barred and precluded" from examining or prosecuting President Trump, his sons and the Trump organization's current tax issues, according to a document posted to the DOJ website.

(Image credit: Julia Demaree Nikhinson)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Minnesota becomes first state to ban prediction markets
item.description: While dozens of states have taken legal action against the controversial industry, Minnesota is the first state to pass a law making it a felony for companies like Kalshi and Polymarket to operate.
item.content:encoded:
While dozens of states have taken legal action against the controversial industry, Minnesota is the first state to pass a law making it a felony for companies like Kalshi and Polymarket to operate.

(Image credit: Steve Karnowski)
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn

item.title: The missing men of the American marriage market
item.description: A new study suggests the growing educational and economic divide between men and women is reshaping marriage and family life in America — leaving many women with a shrinking pool of economically stable partners.
item.content:encoded: A new study suggests the growing educational and economic divide between men and women is reshaping marriage and family life in America — leaving many women with a shrinking pool of economically stable partners.
item.dc:creator: Greg Rosalsky

item.title: Why you should care about 2 power companies merging. Hint: affordability
item.description: NextEra Energy plans to acquire Dominion Energy to create the largest electricity producer in the United States.
item.content:encoded:
NextEra Energy plans to acquire Dominion Energy to create the largest electricity producer in the United States.

(Image credit: Marco Bello)
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha

item.title: The business of storage units is booming. We met Vermont's operators
item.description: Self-storage operators in Vermont report long waitlists and big profits. The state's tight housing market and rapidly aging population contribute to the growth.
item.content:encoded: Self-storage operators in Vermont report long waitlists and big profits. The state's tight housing market and rapidly aging population contribute to the growth.
item.dc:creator: Mikaela Lefrak

item.title: Jury dismisses all claims in Elon Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI CEO Sam Altman
item.description: Musk had sought to oust Altman from his leadership position over claims that he and others breached their duty to OpenAI's original nonprofit mission and unjustly enriched themselves.
item.content:encoded:
Musk had sought to oust Altman from his leadership position over claims that he and others breached their duty to OpenAI's original nonprofit mission and unjustly enriched themselves.

(Image credit: Godofredo A. Vásquez)
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch

item.title: NPR trims jobs in newsroom overhaul as it confronts era without public funding
item.description: NPR is offering buyouts to journalists as it overhauls its newsroom, with the threat of layoffs to follow. Two recent gifts totaling $113 million are primarily dedicated to NPR's tech infrastructure.
item.content:encoded:
NPR is offering buyouts to journalists as it overhauls its newsroom, with the threat of layoffs to follow. Two recent gifts totaling $113 million are primarily dedicated to NPR's tech infrastructure.

(Image credit: Bloomberg via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik

item.title: Judge dismisses Trump's IRS lawsuit, paving the way for a settlement
item.description: The president sued the IRS and the Treasury Department in January, demanding $10 billion over the leak of his tax returns years ago.
item.content:encoded:
The president sued the IRS and the Treasury Department in January, demanding $10 billion over the leak of his tax returns years ago.

(Image credit: Tasos Katopodis)
item.dc:creator: Carrie Johnson

item.title: Why catching insider trading is so tricky nowadays, and just how helpful is it for kids to sleep in?
item.description: Millions of dollars have been made through eerily well-timed bets on prediction markets like Polymarket. We look at why they're so hard to police. And, a new study that supports kids sleeping in.
item.content:encoded: Millions of dollars have been made through eerily well-timed bets on prediction markets like Polymarket. We look at why they're so hard to police. And, a new study that supports kids sleeping in.
item.dc:creator: Cooper Katz McKim

item.title: The business of storage units is booming. We met Vermont's operators
item.description: Self-storage operators in Vermont report long waitlists and big profits. The state's tight housing market and rapidly aging population contribute to the growth.
item.content:encoded: Self-storage operators in Vermont report long waitlists and big profits. The state's tight housing market and rapidly aging population contribute to the growth.
item.dc:creator: Mikaela Lefrak
